news-global-econ.title: Ex-Vikings captain calls on MLB players to double-down on faith amid Pride Night cap controversy
news-global-econ.description: Former Minnesota Vikings captain Jack Brewer urges Giants pitchers to keep writing Bible verses on Pride Night caps after MLB warning, calling it hypocrisy.

news-global-econ.title: Lines wrap around block as customers wait hours for viral dot cakes that sell out within minutes
news-global-econ.description: A mother-daughter business in Roslyn, New York, created Dot Cakes, the viral rainbow sprinkle desserts now selling out and drawing crowds from other countries.

news-global-econ.title: A problem hiding in plain sight is keeping Americans from buying homes
news-global-econ.description: America's construction industry is short 250,000 workers monthly, extending building timelines and driving up costs amid a 1.5 million-home housing gap.

news-global-econ.title: While Mexico defends ‘sovereignty,’ cartels import a flesh-eating parasite into Texas
news-global-econ.description: New World screwworm returns to U.S. soil for the first time in 60 years as cartel-controlled corridors carry infested livestock northward from Mexico.

news-global-econ.title: Apache helicopter gunships showed off their power cleaning up the Strait of Hormuz
news-global-econ.description: AH-64E Apache helicopters play a major role in reopening the Strait of Hormuz, policing lanes for oil tankers despite a June 8 Iranian drone strike.

Spanish far-left MEP Irene Montero of the Podemos Party on Tuesday protested against President Donald Trump at the European Parliament by singing a modified version of the "Happy Birthday" song, calling him "Mister Genocide."

news-global-soc.title: 1 Dead, 1 Wounded, Suspect at Large, After Delaware Hospital Shooting
news-global-soc.description:
One person is reportedly dead, another person wounded, and a suspect is at large following a shooting inside Wilmington Hospital around 3:30 p.m. on Tuesday.
